methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $232M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Lunar Energy is a Series D climate and energy company founded by Kunal Girotra that has raised $232M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech companies and 1 public peer in the climate and energy sector, including stage-adjacent companies like Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $232M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D to next-stage advancement, yielding $348M. The Peer-Median method calculates the median valuation across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables at $1.5B, with a range spanning $700M to $6.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within frontier energy.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $296M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $924M. The Stage-Progression result sits below the peer median, consistent with Lunar Energy's earlier position within the cohort. Key uncertainties include the appropriate peer weighting given Redwood Materials' outsized $6.0B valuation and whether Lunar Energy's technology and market position align more closely with $2B+ peers or sub-$1B peers. Revenue metrics, path to unit economics, and regulatory tailwinds would refine this range materially.
